methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
SunDrive Solar is a Seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised A$60M+ in total funding, including a A$25.3M ARENA grant as of November 2025. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector, including later-stage peers such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses total capital raised (A$60M+) as a proxy base and applies a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implying a $10M valuation. The Peer-Median method references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, Steady Energy at $110M).

These methods triangulate to a broad valuation range of $8.5M to $172.5M, with a median of $80M. The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the tension between SunDrive's early stage and the later-stage funding profile of available comparables. Key uncertainties include the extent to which the ARENA grant (non-dilutive capital) should adjust valuation upward, the appropriate stage classification relative to peers, and execution risk inherent in frontier energy technology. The analysis is most reliable within the $30M–$120M band, where stage progression and peer positioning converge.
